subject: Sports
title: Wilt Chamberlain's 100-Point Game
description: A 60-second lesson on Wilt Chamberlain's 100-Point Game.

hook.text: One player scored 100 points in a single NBA game. That was in 1962, and nobody has scored even 82 since.

buildup.text: On March 2, 1962, Wilt Chamberlain scored 100 points for Philadelphia against the New York Knicks.

discovery.text: He made 36 of 63 field goals and 28 of 32 free throws—remarkable because he was a notoriously poor free-throw shooter.

twist.text: The Knicks tried to slow the game down in the fourth quarter. Wilt's teammates intentionally fouled to get the ball back.

climax.text: Kobe's 81 in 2006 is the closest anyone has come. The gap between 100 and 81 is enormous.

punchline.text: One hundred points. Sixty-three years and counting. Untouchable.

quiz.question: What is the second-highest single-game scoring total in NBA history?
